Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Activities mentioned: - OR: Order Received - MS: Material Sourcing - QS: Quality Screening - AS: Assembly - IQC: In-line Quality Check - TST: Testing - RT: Re-Testing - PK: Packaging - LB: Labeling (may be skipped) - DP: Distribution Planning - D: Dispatch Lets look at the cases. Case 1: OR -> MS -> QS -> AS -> IQC -> AS -> TST -> RT -> TST -> PK -> LB -> DP -> D

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Start with: OR, MS, QS. They always appear in this order. OR -> MS -> QS.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1: AS, IQC, AS Case 2: AS, IQC, AS Case 3: AS, IQC, IQC, AS, IQC, AS Case 4: AS (no IQC) Case 5: AS, IQC, AS, IQC, AS So its a loop between AS and IQC

Name: Validation - NE
Text: But Case 3 has: AS, IQC, IQC, AS, IQC, AS. This means IQC was executed twice in a row? Thats impossible with *(AS, IQC) because the loop alternates AS and IQC.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Ah! IQC: In-line Quality Check (repeated if needed) This suggests IQC itself might be in a loop, or the check is repeated. Maybe the loop is *(AS, loop_IQC)? loop_IQC = *(IQC, tau). Then the main loop is *(AS, loop_IQC).

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wow, this is a very elegant interpretation! ... Result: AS, IQC, IQC, AS, IQC, AS. Exactly matches Case 3! ... Matches Case 5 perfectly!

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1: TST, RT, TST Case 2: TST Case 3: TST, RT, TST Case 4: TST Case 5: TST, RT, TST, RT, TST This is clearly a loop between TST and RT.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1: PK, LB Case 2: PK (no LB) ... So LB can be skipped. This means there is an XOR choice between LB and a silent transition (tau).

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the overall flow is: OR -> MS -> QS -> loop_AS_IQC -> loop_TST_RT -> PK -> xor_LB -> DP -> D ... root = StrictPartialOrder(nodes=[OR, MS, QS, loop_AS_IQC, loop_TST_RT, PK, xor_LB, DP, D]) root.order.add_edge(OR, MS) ...

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: What if *(X(AS, IQC), tau)? ... But in the log, does IQC ever start a case? AS is always the first activity after QS. Therefore, *(AS, loop_IQC) is much more precise

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Everything aligns perfectly. The nested loop structure correctly handles the IQC: In-line Quality Check (repeated if needed) logic
